name: LEGO
description: The Danish toy empire — bricks, minifigures, and the little plastic studs that have been everywhere since 1949.

questions.q: In which country was the LEGO Group founded?

source.notes: Founded in Billund, Denmark.

questions.q: The name 'LEGO' comes from the Danish phrase 'leg godt,' meaning what?

source.notes: Coincidentally, 'lego' can also be read as Latin for 'I put together.'

questions.q: Which Danish carpenter-turned-toymaker founded the LEGO Group in 1932?

source.notes: Started making wooden toys during the Great Depression.

questions.q: LEGO began producing what iconic product in 1949?

source.notes: Originally called 'Automatic Binding Bricks.'

questions.q: When did LEGO first introduce its iconic minifigure — the little yellow-faced person?

source.notes: First modern minifigure: a police officer.

questions.q: The chunky, larger-scale LEGO product line for toddlers is called what?

source.original_a: Twice the size of standard Lego bricks
source.notes: 'DUPLO' — bricks are twice the size of standard LEGO.

questions.q: Which 2014 animated feature launched a wildly successful LEGO movie franchise?

source.notes: Directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller.

questions.q: LEGO's modern brick design — still compatible with bricks made today — was patented in which year?

source.notes: The stud-and-tube coupling system.

questions.q: Since 1963, LEGO bricks have been made primarily from which plastic?

source.notes: Replaced cellulose acetate.

questions.q: Which 1960 event pushed LEGO to abandon wooden-toy production entirely?

source.notes: The 1960 Billund warehouse fire destroyed stock of wooden toys.

questions.q: LEGO's founding motto, coined by Ole Kirk Christiansen, is what?

source.notes: In Danish: 'Kun det bedste er godt nok.'

questions.q: What Denmark-based theme park attraction, the original of its kind, opened in 1968?

source.notes: LEGOLAND Billund opened 1968.

questions.q: The LEGO Technic line launched in 1977 introduced what new type of element to the brick system?

source.notes: Technic uses pegs, gears, and beams rather than studs.

questions.q: Which Japanese video-game franchise launched its own LEGO set theme in 2020, featuring an interactive figure?

source.notes: LEGO Super Mario debuted in August 2020 with an electronic Mario figure.

questions.q: The LEGO brick is said to have a clutch tolerance as small as how many micrometers?

source.notes: Crazy-tight tolerance for backward compatibility.

questions.q: LEGO's headquarters remain in which small Danish town, also the site of the first LEGOLAND?

source.original_a: Billund, Nyíregyháza, Monterrey, and Jiaxing, China
source.notes: Billund also hosts the LEGO House, opened 2017.

questions.q: The second-ever LEGOLAND park opened in 1996 outside which major UK city?

source.notes: LEGOLAND Windsor.

questions.q: LEGO licensed a popular sci-fi franchise in 1999 that remains one of its most successful themes. Which was it?

source.notes: Timed to The Phantom Menace; revitalized LEGO's finances.

questions.q: By roughly what year did the LEGO Group become the largest toy maker in the world by revenue, overtaking Mattel and Hasbro?

source.notes: LEGO topped the global toy charts around 2014-15.

questions.q: Which 2017 LEGO House in Billund was designed by which celebrated Danish architect?

source.notes: BIG (Bjarke Ingels Group).

questions.q: A 2015 estimate placed cumulative LEGO brick production at roughly how many bricks?

source.notes: About 80 bricks for every person on Earth.

questions.q: LEGO has announced plans to make bricks out of sugarcane-based PE for which product subset first?

source.notes: Bio-PE rolled out for botanical pieces in 2018.
